name: Lenny Rachitsky Podcast Transcripts MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server providing structured access to Lenny Rachitsky podcast transcripts. Enables AI agents to search and retrieve content from Lenny's Podcast — a popular product management and startup podcast — enabling Q&A, summarization, and knowledge extraction from the episode archive.
use_cases: Searching Lenny Rachitsky podcast transcripts for specific product management topics
Extracting insights and advice from specific episodes for AI research agents
Building Q&A systems over Lenny's podcast archive for learning applications
Generating thematic summaries across multiple episodes for topic researchers
not_for: Commercial redistribution of podcast content (copyright restrictions)
Non-product management topics (Lenny's podcast is PM/startup focused)
best_when: A product manager or startup professional wants AI agents to retrieve and reason over Lenny Rachitsky's podcast archive — extracting advice, finding relevant episodes, or synthesizing insights across interviews.
avoid_when: You need content from other podcasts. Also: podcast transcripts may have transcription errors — verify quotes before using in publications.

auth.notes: HAPI MCP framework. Podcast transcript access terms — verify Lenny's Newsletter licensing before commercial use.

pricing.notes: HAPI MCP framework service. Transcript data licensing from Lenny's Newsletter applies.

known_agent_gotchas: Transcript data coverage depends on episodes available — not all episodes may be transcribed
Transcription accuracy varies — AI transcripts may have errors in technical terms
Copyright: podcast transcripts have licensing restrictions — verify before commercial use
HAPI intermediary: data passes through HAPI MCP framework service
Dataset freshness: verify whether new episodes are indexed in near-real-time or with delay
agent_readiness.security_notes: Podcast content is generally public. Copyright compliance for commercial use. HAPI framework data handling policies apply.

security_score_components.security_notes: Podcast transcripts are generally low-sensitivity. Copyright compliance required. HAPI framework intermediary. HTTPS.
